title: The Alttene Playbook: One Product, Two Initiatives, Full Attention
excerpt: Why we run a product, a consultancy, and a giving-back platform under one small roof — and how we keep them feeding each other.
content:
The standard advice for a small studio is focus: do one thing. We run three — Koffeechat, Alto Partners, and Fisayo.org — and we'd argue it's still one thing, wearing three outfits.

The flywheel

Each part of Alttene sharpens the others:

  1. Building teaches. Shipping Koffeechat generates real operating lessons — pricing mistakes, retention wins, migration scars.

  2. Advising tests. Alto Partners takes those lessons into other people's companies, where they either survive contact with reality or get revised.

  3. Giving grounds. Fisayo.org keeps the whole machine pointed at the reason we started: more people getting into rooms they'd otherwise never see.

The rules we run by

  • Small on purpose. Headcount is a cost, focus is the asset.

  • Everything starts with a conversation. Products, engagements, hires — if we can't talk it through over a coffee, we're not ready to start it.

  • Ship it well made. Craft is the brand. There is no marketing budget that beats a thing that obviously had care put into it.

That's the playbook. We'll report back from the field as we run it.
author: Alttene Team
